data.content: K-34 plan (strict mode): 1) Remove value <input> from create/update forms in template.html — replace value column with a <code> snippet showing 'thimble set <app> <env> <KEY>'. 2) Server: in handleSecret, if action is create or update and FormValue('value') != '', return 400 with body 'web UI does not accept secret values; use the CLI: thimble set <app> <env> <KEY>'. Do NOT echo back the submitted value. 3) Keep delete form; keep recipient form (recipients are public). 4) Tests: strict-mode POST returns 400; rendered HTML contains no <input name="value"> for any secret create/update form (delete still allowed); rendered HTML contains 'thimble set' suggestion. 5) Update README 'Web UI' section and SECURITY.md to describe the redacted-viewer + recipient/key-manager scope.
